meaning: adj.贪欲的,渴望的,好色的,淫荡的
examples: A country which tends towards primness by day is often alcohol-fueled and lustful by night.
That made her perfect, Unilever concluded, to draw in the 18- to 24-year-old lustful lads to whom Axe is shamelessly marketed.
I think we are lustful for power, for this, or for that, but we seem to have lost, or never had this passion for doing the correct thing, doing the good thing.
Haas is irresistibly sly and rapt as an adolescent whose lustful curiosity turns to love.
His Scarpia, the lustful and unscrupulous chief of the Roman police, remains among opera's most indelible villains a model for most of his successors in this role.
